base_context.description: The next run will be this leader/base config plus ONLY the JSON changes returned by the advisor.

expected_direction: increase net profit or PnL per exposure hour without worsening MAE
expected_effect: This should keep charm_wall_breakaway_long as a high-convexity VALID_0DTE profile while testing tighter structural admission and a smaller max TP envelope. Expected benefit is better risk-adjusted efficiency and no increase in MAE or slow exposure. Main tradeoff is under-monetizing the rare 12-point wall-breakaway winners; reject if profile PnL/hour falls below about 225, total net profit drops more than 2% from run_044, or completed trades fall more than 10% without material objective improvement.
label: promote
rationale: Choose Batch D because the base run_044 is the score leader and the latest run_049 is a rejected ADX-period branch, so the next valid experiment should be profile-specific rather than another global entry perturbation. The charm_wall_breakaway_long edge is small-sample but very efficient and protected by the strategist plan. This is a legal one-family test using Batch D keys, avoids missing/stale 0DTE behavior, avoids broad veto tightening, and does not repeat the recently rejected VWAP-cap geometry or call-wall TP compression experiments.
